result.summary: HubSpot exposes one HTTP API at https://api.hubapi.com/, plus two MCP servers: remote HubSpot MCP at https://mcp.hubspot.com and a local Developer MCP configured via `hs mcp setup`; auth is OAuth for the API/remote MCP and CLI login for Developer MCP.

hubspot_oauth_app.label: HubSpot OAuth app
hubspot_oauth_app.setup: Create a developer account on HubSpot, then create an app in the developer platform and configure its OAuth credentials. For the remote MCP server, you use the app’s OAuth client to authorize access to CRM data. HubSpot notes that MCP clients must support PKCE and refresh-token rotation. Start from the developer platform docs and the MCP integration guide: [Developer platform](https://developers.hubspot.com/) and [MCP server guide](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/apps/developer-platform/build-apps/integrate-with-hubspot-mcp-server).

hubspot_cli_login.label: HubSpot CLI login
hubspot_cli_login.setup: Install the HubSpot CLI and run `hs login` to authenticate the CLI for local developer tooling and the developer MCP server. The MCP setup guide says the local developer MCP server is configured via `hs mcp setup` after installing the CLI. See [CLI install](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/developer-tooling/local-development/hubspot-cli/install-the-cli) and [Developer MCP setup](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/developer-tooling/local-development/mcp-server).

surfaces.name: HubSpot CRM / Developer Platform HTTP API
